Change: Fix .gitignore: uncomment .env exclusion + add sensitive file patterns. Remove 6 sensitive files from git tracking via git rm --cached. Add root .gitignore for monorepo.
Replace cosmetic-only auth check on /uploads/* (only checked header existence) with real authenticateJwtOrApiKey middleware that verifies JWT signature or validates API key
Add per-route rate limits: login 5/min, register 3/min, forgot-password 3/5min, reset-password 3/5min. Uses @fastify/rate-limit config field (already registered globally)
1) Gate Swagger UI behind NODE_ENV !== production (completely disabled in prod). 2) Fix CORS: origin callback validates against comma-separated allowlist instead of single string pass-through

Refresh tokens now signed+verified with separate config.jwt.refreshSecret (via jsonwebtoken package) instead of same secret as access tokens. Compromised access token secret no longer leaks refresh tokens.
1) bcrypt rounds 10→12 (OWASP). 2) Password complexity: min 8 chars + 1 uppercase + 1 lowercase + 1 digit. Applied to register, resetPassword, changePassword schemas.
Migrated 76 console.log + 17 console.error/warn → structured Pino logger (logger.info/debug/warn/error). Only 2 console.warn remain in config/index.ts (boot-time, before logger init — acceptable).
Recursive processBroadcast() → while(true) loop. Prevents stack overflow with 10k+ recipients. Added null-safety on delay: ?? 1000 / ?? 3000.

File(s): backend/src/config/redis-connection.ts (NEW), broadcast.worker.ts, webhook.worker.ts, daily-reset.worker.ts, webhooks.service.ts, broadcasts.service.ts
Change: Created shared redis-connection.ts config. All 5 files now import from shared config instead of hardcoding Redis host/port/password.
WebhookService(null as any) → WebhookService() (no arg). Constructor param made optional — fastify is never used in class body.
Removed dead multi-tenant Prisma $use middleware — organizationId is never set on params, 100% dead code giving false sense of isolation.
Refresh tokens now hashed with SHA-256 before DB storage. register/login store hash, refreshToken compares hash.
Reset tokens now hashed with SHA-256. forgotPassword stores hash, resetPassword queries by hash.
manuallyDisconnecting cleanup: socket.logout() in try/finally, socket.end() in try/finally. Set always cleaned on error/success.
Fire-and-forget .catch(() => {}) → proper error logging with request.log.warn / logger.debug.
Upload size validation: toBuffer() before size check ensures full stream consumed. Prevents oversized file bypass.
Shutdown signal handlers wrapped in try/catch. If shutdownWorkers() throws, fastify.close() still runs.
Removed dead code: verifyEmailSchema (unused), resetDailyMessageCounts (duplicate of worker), .bak file.
SKIPPED: Double validation (JSON Schema + Zod) kept intentionally — removing JSON Schema would break Swagger docs in dev mode.

Unified error handling: 8 modules' local handleError() + errorCodeMap removed. Services throw AppError with proper statusCode/code. Errors flow to global errorHandler. Fixed 7 bugs where errors fell to 500. Fixed ZodError interception (was 500, now proper 400).
Server-side route protection via Next.js middleware. /dashboard/** requires accessToken cookie. /login + /register redirect to /dashboard if authenticated. Login respects ?redirect= param.
Fixed stale cache: queryKeys.instances and webhookLogs now include params. Fixed docs page using wrong env var (NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL → import from constants).
Added React error boundaries at root and dashboard level. Shows user-friendly error UI with retry button.
Fixed URL.revokeObjectURL memory leak: empty deps [] captured initial empty files. Now uses filesRef to track current files for unmount cleanup.
Removed dead UI: Remember Me checkbox (state never consumed), Search bar (no handler/state), Notification bell (no handler/no system, static fake red dot).

File(s): .gitignore, cararunredis.txt, pashe.txt, saran dari temen saya.txt
Change: Deleted 3 junk files (cararunredis.txt contained exposed API keys!). Added saran file + *.bak pattern to .gitignore.
QR timing fix: useInstanceQr staleTime: 0, gcTime: 0, retry: 1. Backend QR expiry 30s→120s. Frontend poll 30s→5s.
useInstanceQr enabled: !!id → enabled: !!id && status === 'QR_READY'. Prevents premature 400 error that poisons React Query cache.
Error handler: recognize @fastify/rate-limit 429 errors instead of falling to 500. Rate limit config: 100/15m → 1000/1m (frontend polls 30 req/min).
initializeActiveInstances: added QR_READY to startup query. Prevents dead state where instance stuck in QR_READY with no QR in memory after server restart.

Safety net fix: sync-status endpoint no longer immediately kills SYNCING state on momentary disconnect. Added 5-minute staleness threshold (checks last_batch_at/started_at). Only auto-corrects to STOPPED if genuinely stale (>5 min + disconnected). Prevents premature sync corruption.
Fix progress.percentage never calculated during sync. Now computed as Math.min(95, batches_received * 2.5) on each batch (matching frontend heuristic). Only completion sets 100%.

Reconnect recovery: connection='open' handler now detects auto-stopped syncs (stopped_reason='stale_auto_corrected') and resets to SYNCING. Removes stopped_at/stopped_reason, adds recovered_at. Allows sync to resume after temporary disconnect.

Fix { set: null } on Json? field → Prisma.DbNull. Old code stored literal JSON {"set":null} instead of SQL NULL, causing: (1) clear sync data button appears to work but progress field is never truly null, (2) needs_repair detection fails (requires progress === null), (3) UI shows inconsistent state. Fixed 3 occurrences + removed band-aid 'delete progress.set' workaround.

Fix sync stuck at SYNCING forever. Two fixes: (1) sync-status endpoint: if SYNCING + CONNECTED + no batch for >2min, auto-complete (lost completion timer from restart). (2) Startup stale cleanup: replaced updateMany(→PARTIAL) with per-instance finalization: sets percentage=100, completed_at, last_history_sync_at, and COMPLETED vs PARTIAL based on batch_errors. Threshold 10min→2min.

Reconnect resilience: (1) badSession disconnect now schedules reconnect (was: mark ERROR + give up forever). (2) All reconnects use exponential backoff 5s→10s→20s→40s→60s with max 10 attempts (was: single attempt, no retry). (3) Startup initializeActiveInstances includes ERROR status in query (was: ignored). (4) Health check zombie detection force-closes socket after 120s of no frames and triggers reconnect (was: warn only).

Fix P2002 unique constraint race condition on message upsert. Prisma upsert does SELECT→INSERT which races when sendText and Baileys messages.upsert event fire concurrently for the same outgoing message. Added safeMessageUpsert() helper that catches P2002 and retries as update. Applied to all 4 upsert call sites (3 in whatsapp.service.ts: sendText, sendMedia, sendLocation; 1 in baileys.service.ts: handleRealtimeMessage).

Auto-detect WA account name. socket.user.name was available from Baileys at runtime but NOT persisted to DB. Added wa_display_name column to WhatsAppInstance schema, saved in connection=open handler, displayed in frontend instance cards and detail page.

Fix wa_display_name always null + phone_number :device suffix. Two bugs: (1) socket.user.name = saved contact name (always undefined for self). Correct field = socket.user.notify (profile name set by owner). Changed to notify → verifiedName → name fallback chain. (2) extractPhoneFromJid did not strip :device suffix from socket.user.id (e.g. 6282119499306:54 → 6282119499306). Result: wa_display_name now 'Adi Syahadi', phone_number now clean.

Expose wa_display_name in all API responses + webhook. Added wa_display_name to: getInstances select, getInstance select, external API list select, connection event emit, webhook connection payload. Without this, the field existed in DB but was never returned to consumers.

Fix getInitials crash: contact.name=null AND contact.phone_number=null (LID-only contacts) → getInitials(null) → Cannot read properties of null (reading 'split'). Added null guard returning '?' fallback. Also added .filter(Boolean) to handle empty-string words.

Fix QR endpoint 400 errors. Root cause: useEffect called refetchQR() which bypasses React Query's enabled guard. When stale cached status=QR_READY triggers the effect but backend has status=DISCONNECTED → 400. Fix: replaced manual useEffect+refetchQR polling with refetchInterval in useInstanceQr hook (respects enabled flag). Removed the QR auto-refresh useEffect entirely.

Fix LIDPhone mapping capture: code listened for non-existent Baileys event 'lid-mapping.update'  replaced with real 'chats.phoneNumberShare' event. Also extract LIDPhone cross-references from 'contacts.upsert' Contact objects (lid/jid/id fields). Added source parameter to handleLidMappingUpdate. Added ev.process() backup handlers for both events. Without this fix, lid_phone_mappings table stayed empty (0 rows) and all @lid private chat webhooks had phone_number=null.

Resolve LIDPhone in webhook payload. In handleRealtimeMessage(), when chatJid ends with @lid, lookup lid_phone_mappings table to resolve phone_number before emitting webhook. Without this, CRM receives phone_number=null for all @lid private chats (87.5% of contacts) and cannot identify customers. Uses existing prisma.lidPhoneMapping.findUnique() with the instance_id_lid_jid composite key. Falls back gracefully with debug log if no mapping exists yet.

Active LID-Phone resolution via onWhatsApp. PATCH-044 only checked DB cache which was empty. Now when no DB mapping exists for an @lid chat, actively queries WhatsApp via sock.onWhatsApp(lid) to resolve the phone JID. Result is cached in lid_phone_mappings for future fast lookups and backfills contact phone_number. This ensures CRM gets phone_number even on first message from a new @lid contact. Fallback chain: DB cache -> onWhatsApp query -> null.

Add contact_name to webhook payload. Webhook message events were missing sender name making CRM unable to display contact names. Fix: extract msg.pushName (WA profile name) from real-time messages and include as contact_name field in webhook payload. Fallback chain: pushName -> Contact.push_name -> Contact.name -> null. Also updates Contact.push_name from incoming messages to keep names fresh.

Download & save incoming media files. Added downloadAndSaveMedia() function that uses Baileys downloadMediaMessage() to download media from incoming IMAGE/VIDEO/AUDIO/DOCUMENT/STICKER messages, then saves to disk via existing saveFile() storage service. Media URL and mimetype stored in DB Message.media_url and Message.media_type columns (already existed in schema but were never populated). Non-critical: download failure does not block message processing. Reuses existing storage.service saveFile() and ensureUploadsDir(). Only downloads for incoming messages (fromMe=false).

Include media_url in webhook payload. When incoming media is downloaded (PATCH-047), the URL is now included in the webhook message event as media_url field. CRM can use this URL to display/download the media file. Uses savedMediaUrl from PATCH-047. Field is undefined (omitted from JSON) for non-media messages to maintain backward compatibility.

Frontend media display in messages table. Replaced static [Media] placeholder with actual media rendering: IMAGE/STICKER shows thumbnail with click-to-open, VIDEO shows icon+link, AUDIO shows mini player, DOCUMENT shows download link with icon. Falls back to icon+type label when media_url is not yet available. Text messages unchanged. No UX disruption - same table layout with richer content column.

Fix 2 bugs in PATCH-047 downloadAndSaveMedia: (1) reuploadRequest always undefined because activeSockets.get(msg.key.remoteJid ? "" : "") always resolved to empty string key. Fixed by adding instanceId parameter and using activeSockets.get(instanceId) for correct socket lookup. Without this fix media re-upload from WA servers would always fail. (2) Removed unused import config from ../../config that was accidentally added in PATCH-047 but never referenced (all config.xxx in file are local variables).

Fix sticker detection in ephemeral and viewOnce message wrappers. extractMessageContent() had two wrapper handlers (ephemeralMessage and viewOnceMessage) that did not handle stickerMessage. Stickers in disappearing chats fell to [Disappearing Message] type TEXT and stickers in viewOnce fell to [View Once Message] type TEXT. Now both correctly detect stickerMessage and set type=STICKER. This enables download+save+webhook+frontend render for wrapped stickers.

File(s): Add public /media/* endpoint (no auth) + change downloadAndSaveMedia URL from /uploads/ to /media/. CRM webhook consumers can now access media_url without 401. UUID v4 filenames = unguessable capability URL.
Change: COMPLETED

File(s): Root cause: decorateReply:false in @fastify/static registration removed reply.sendFile() from all routes. Fix: remove decorateReply:false (default=true). Both /uploads/* and /media/* now serve files correctly.
Change: COMPLETED

File(s): Root cause: initializeConnection() cleared reconnectTimers but NOT reconnectAttempts. After a loggedOut(401) cycle consumed 10 attempts, manual Connect gets only 1 attempt then hits MAX. Fix: add reconnectAttempts.delete(instanceId) at start of initializeConnection().
Change: COMPLETED
